authentication_mode: First-party claude.ai authentication using the persisted user session; credentials were not inspected or exported
approval_mode: plan with only the built-in Read tool enabled
sandbox_mode: No Windows sandbox is claimed; the tool surface is restricted to Read and customizations are disabled by --safe-mode
notes: Anthropic's official native installer installed Claude Code 2.1.210 at the explicit user-scope executable path.
Local help verified --safe-mode, --strict-mcp-config, --tools, --permission-mode plan, --no-session-persistence, --output-format json, --add-dir, and -p.
The validated Test A gate disabled all built-in tools. Tests B and C enable only Read without enabling discovery, shell, or write tools.
Anthropic documents HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, NO_PROXY, and NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS; interception and CA trust were validated for the recorded Test A, Test B, and Test C runs and remain run-specific.
limitations: Successful authenticated Test A, Test B, and Test C executions established the persisted first-party session without inspecting account details or credential material.
Claude Code documentation states that sandboxing is not supported on native Windows, so no OS sandbox guarantee is claimed.
Proxy routing, certificate trust, expected hosts, and direct-bypass behavior must be validated independently for each approved live run.
The recorded Test A, Test B, and Test C results apply only to their preserved commands, prompts, environments, and evidence.
